tool_args.label: LIRR Fall Trackwork Programs & Holiday Services
tool_args.raw_text: Subject FALL TRACKWORK PROGRAMS & HOLIDAY SERVICE Date October 6, 2022 Departments SR. VICE PRESIDENT – OPERATIONS ... This is to inform the Long Island Committee of the MTA Long Island Rail Road's plan to provide added service for the 2022 Thanksgiving Holiday.

payload.title: LIRR Fall Trackwork Programs & Holiday Services
payload.description: Staff summary informing the Long Island Committee of MTA LIRR's plan for Fall 2022 trackwork programs and Thanksgiving Holiday service.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Queens Reconfiguration Project
tool_args.raw_text: Signal Cutover in Queens Interlocking as part of the Queens Reconfiguration Project

payload.description: Signal cutover in Queens Interlocking as part of the Queens Reconfiguration Project. All main tracks on the Main Line out of service between Queens and Hicksville for approximately 50 hours on the weekend of October 29-30, 2022.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Signal Cutover in Queens Interlocking as part of the Queens Reconfiguration Project

all main tracks on the Main Line will be out of service between Queens and Hicksville for approximately 50 hours for a signal cutover in Queens Interlocking as part of the Queens Reconfiguration Project

tool_args.label: Great Neck Pocket Extension
tool_args.raw_text: Switch Installation, Track Relocation, and a Signal Cutover in support of the Great Neck Pocket Extension

payload.description: Switch installation, track relocation and a signal cutover on the Port Washington Branch. All tracks out of service between Bayside and Port Washington for approximately 48 hours on weekends of November 5-6, 12-13, and 19-20, 2022. Weekday work November 7-11 and 14-18, 2022 near the Great Neck Pocket Track.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Great Neck Pocket Extension
all tracks on the Port Washington Branch will be out of service between Bayside and Port Washington for approximately 48 hours for a switch installation, track relocation and a signal cutover

tool_args.label: Hempstead Branch Concrete Tie Installation and Rail Replacement
tool_args.raw_text: Concrete Tie and Rail Replacement on the Hempstead Branch

payload.project_name: Hempstead Branch Concrete Tie Installation and Rail Replacement
payload.project_type: concrete tie installation and rail replacement
payload.description: Concrete tie installation and rail replacement on the Hempstead Branch. Both main tracks out of service for approximately 48 hours on weekend of November 12-13, 2022. Originally scheduled for October 15-16. New concrete ties will offer greater reliability versus traditional wooden ties and a longer lifespan.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Concrete Tie and Rail Replacement on the Hempstead Branch
both main tracks on the Hempstead Branch will be out of service for approximately 48 hours while new concrete ties are installed, and the existing rail is replaced
Installing the new concrete ties will offer greater reliability versus traditional wooden ties, and a longer lifespan, thus reducing the need for future outages

tool_args.label: Main Line Signal Cutover Weekend
tool_args.raw_text: On the weekend of October 29-30, all main tracks on the Main Line will be out of service between Queens and Hicksville for approximately 50 hours for a signal cutover in Queens Interlocking as part of the Queens Reconfiguration Project.

payload.description: Weekend track outage for signal cutover in Queens Interlocking. All main tracks on the Main Line out of service between Queens and Hicksville for approximately 50 hours. Part of the Queens Reconfiguration Project.

tool_args.label: Port Washington Branch Great Neck Pocket Extension Weekends
tool_args.raw_text: On the weekends of November 5-6, 12-13, and 19-20, all tracks on the Port Washington Branch will be out of service between Bayside and Port Washington for approximately 48 hours for a switch installation, track relocation and a signal cutover.

payload.event_name: Port Washington Branch Great Neck Pocket Extension Work
payload.date_text: Weekends of November 5-6, 12-13, and 19-20, 2022; weekdays November 7-11 and 14-18, 2022
payload.description: Weekend and weekday work for switch installation, track relocation and signal cutover. All tracks out of service between Bayside and Port Washington for approximately 48 hours each weekend. In support of the Great Neck Pocket Extension.

date_text_normalized.raw_text: Weekends of November 5-6, 12-13, and 19-20, 2022; weekdays November 7-11 and 14-18, 2022

tool_args.label: Hempstead Branch Concrete Tie Installation Weekend
tool_args.raw_text: On the weekend of November 12-13, both main tracks on the Hempstead Branch will be out of service for approximately 48 hours while new concrete ties are installed, and the existing rail is replaced. This outage was rescheduled from October 15-16.

payload.event_name: Hempstead Branch Concrete Tie Installation and Rail Replacement
payload.description: Weekend track outage for concrete tie installation and rail replacement on the Hempstead Branch. Both main tracks out of service for approximately 48 hours. Rescheduled from October 15-16.

tool_args.label: Thanksgiving Eve Program 2022
tool_args.raw_text: The LIRR will operate a regular weekday schedule with additional service added. The Thanksgiving Eve program includes the standard 11 early release afternoon eastbound trains for people leaving the city early, plus an additional Montauk Branch service opportunity from Penn Station.

payload.description: LIRR operates regular weekday schedule with additional service: 11 early release afternoon eastbound trains plus an additional Montauk Branch service from Penn Station. Four extra trains to Babylon, two to Huntington, two to Ronkonkoma, one each to Hicksville, Great Neck, Far Rockaway and Montauk.

tool_args.label: Thanksgiving Day Program 2022
tool_args.raw_text: The Thanksgiving Day program includes six extra westbound trains on Thanksgiving morning for those attending the parade in NYC, and 11 early afternoon eastbound extra trains for those returning home from the parade or heading to Long Island for Thanksgiving gatherings.

payload.description: LIRR operates six extra westbound trains on Thanksgiving morning for parade attendees, and 11 early afternoon eastbound extra trains for those returning home or heading to Long Island.

tool_args.label: Concrete ties greater reliability claim
tool_args.raw_text: Installing the new concrete ties will offer greater reliability versus traditional wooden ties, and a longer lifespan, thus reducing the need for future outages.

payload.claim_text: Installing the new concrete ties will offer greater reliability versus traditional wooden ties, and a longer lifespan, thus reducing the need for future outages.
payload.description: Claim that concrete ties offer greater reliability, longer lifespan, and reduce future outage needs compared to wooden ties on the Hempstead Branch.
payload.subject: Hempstead Branch

evidence_refs.source_quote: Installing the new concrete ties will offer greater reliability versus traditional wooden ties, and a longer lifespan, thus reducing the need for future outages.
